title: High Dose STZ Induction Protocol
author: Frank Brosius
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8iyhufw

steps.instruction: Prepare the buffers and solutions as described below. Please note that the STZ- Na-Citrate solution should be prepared immediately prior to injection so as to avoid degradation of the STZ.
Mice should be fasted prior to injection; four hours is usually sufficient.
Place one mouse in the Isoflurane drop jar, according to your local IACUC anesthesia standards.
Remove mouse when breathing has slowed and animal is anesthetized
Inject appropriate amount of the STZ solution IP so the final dosage is 150 mg/kg mouse
Allow mouse to awaken and place back in cage
Repeat procedure for each animal; keep in mind that Isoflurane will need to be re- loaded after every third or fourth animal for proper anesthesia.
Each mouse should be given one injection.
Supply mice with 10% sucrose water overnight to avoid sudden hypoglycemia post-injection.
Mice should be tested for sufficient levels of hyperglycemia two days after injection and 4 weeks post-injection. Mice should be severely diabetic.
